title: fagraea dulitensis
intro: Fagraea dulitensis, also known as the Borneo fragrance tree or Kadupul flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Borneo and is known for its unique and fragrant flowers that bloom at night and wilt before dawn. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The leaves and flowers of the Fagraea dulitensis plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are also used in traditional remedies to promote health and well-being.
